center.name: 伊斯帕尼奥拉岛
center.summary: 伊斯帕尼奥拉岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联14种鸟类，包括山齒鶉、美洲紅鸛、White-crowned pigeon等14种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Bicknell's and gray-cheeked thrushes, along with the veery, make up a close-knit group of migrant species. The birds migrate to the Greater Antilles, with an estimated 90% of the individuals wintering in Hispaniola (mostly in the Dominican Republic, but also Haiti).{{cite journal|url=http://www.soh.org.do/Palmchatter1_2%20English.pdf |title=Bicknell's Thrush Conservation—Are Females Getting Squeezed?

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the Bahamas, Hispaniola (the Dominican Republic and Haiti, as well as surrounding islands), Jamaica, and the Turks and Caicos Islands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The loggerhead kingbird is found in throughout the northern West Indies, in the Bahamas, Cayman Islands, Cuba, Isla de la Juventud, Hispaniola (the Dominican Republic and Haiti), Jamaica and Puerto Rico. It very rarely occurs in southern Florida as a vagrant.{{cite web |url=https://www.birds-of-north-america.net/Loggerhead_Kingbird.html#:~:text=The%20Loggerhead%20Kingbird%20is%20a,but%20displays%20a%20bulkier%20body

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the four Greater Antilles, on Cuba, Hispaniola (in the Dominican Republic and Haiti), Jamaica, and Puerto Rico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Mexico, parts of Central America, Cuba, Hispaniola, and 10 countries in South America.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/owls/ |title=Owls |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 12.1 |editor-last1=Gill |editor-first1= F

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Cuba and Hispaniola (split between the Dominican Republic and Haiti)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in smaller numbers in Hispaniola (the Dominican Republic and Haiti), Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, the Cayman Islands, Anguilla and other Caribbean islands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is absent from the southern tip of Florida (where the extinct Key West bobwhite subspecies once lived) and the highest elevations of the Appalachian Mountains, but occurs in eastern Mexico and in Cuba, and has been introduced to Hispaniola (both the Dominican Republic and Haiti), the Bahamas, the Turks and Caicos Islands, the U.S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Cory|Cory]], 1883) | synonyms = Rupornis ridgwayi | range_map = Buteo ridgwayi map.svg | range_map_caption = Range in green }} Ridgway's hawk (Buteo ridgwayi) is a bird of prey in the family Accipitridae endemic to the island of Hispaniola (both Haiti and the Dominican Republic) in the Caribbean

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: galbula Note: range in Venezuela, Hispaniola, Puerto Rico, and the rest of the Caribbean not shown | synonyms = *Coracias galbula *Oriolus galbula }} The Baltimore oriole (Icterus galbula) is a small icterid blackbird common in eastern North America as a migratory breeding bird

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: capensis }} The rufous-collared sparrow or Andean sparrow (Zonotrichia capensis) is an American sparrow found in a wide range of habitats, often near humans, from the extreme south-east of Mexico to Tierra del Fuego, and the island of Hispaniola in the Caribbean.{{cite book |title=Birds of Aruba, Bonaire, and Curacao: A Site and Field Guide |author1=Jeffrey V

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The American flamingo breeds in South America (in the Galápagos Islands of Ecuador, coastal Colombia and Venezuela, and northern Brazil), in the West Indies (Trinidad and Tobago, Cuba, Jamaica, Hispaniola (the Dominican Republic and Haiti), The Bahamas, the Virgin Islands, and the Turks and Caicos Islands), and tropical and subtropical areas of continental North America (along the northern coast of the Yucatán Peninsula in Mexico, and formerly southern Florida in the United States)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In recent years, breeding pairs have been found in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan, Wisconsin, and southern Ontario due to the expanding population. The birds winter in Cuba, The Bahamas and the nearby Turks and Caicos Islands, where they are found on all islands investigated. A number also overwinter on Hispaniola, in the Dominican Republic.{{cite journal |last1=Faanes |first1=Craig A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It has also been introduced to Portugal and Venezuela as well as to the islands of Hispaniola, Martinique, Puerto Rico, Mauritius and Réunion. This often abundant species occurs in a wide range of open or semi-open habitats, including woodlands and human habitation, and frequently forms large noisy colonies in towns, villages, and hotel grounds
